dosing_enrichment.titration_note: Introduce MOTS-c at 5 mg 3x/week from week 1 (no titration needed at standard dose). If SS-31 included, start at 20 mg/day weeks 1-2, increase to 40 mg/day week 3+. Monitor fatigue VAS score weekly for first 4 weeks.
dosing_enrichment.cycling_recommendation: 12-week active cycle followed by 4-week washout. MOTS-c receptor sensitivity is maintained with cycling; SS-31 mitochondrial stabilization benefits persist post-cycle.
dosing_enrichment.maintenance_dose: MOTS-c: 5 mg subcutaneous 3x/week (Mon/Wed/Fri). SS-31 (Elamipretide): 40 mg/day subcutaneous if available. NAD+ precursor (NMN 500 mg oral) as adjunct daily.
dosing_enrichment.timing_optimization: MOTS-c best administered fasted or 90 min pre-exercise to leverage AMPK activation during metabolic demand. SS-31 morning subcutaneous. NMN with morning meal.

protocol_author_notes: Official curated protocol for general clinical use.

qualitative: Improved sustained energy and reduction in post-exertional fatigue
Improved exercise tolerance and recovery speed
Reduction in inflammatory biomarkers linked to mitochondrial dysfunction

quantitative_ranges.week_8: Exercise endurance improvement 15-20% (6-minute walk test or cycling protocol); IL-6 reduction 15-25%; HOMA-IR improvement 20-30%
quantitative_ranges.week_4: VAS fatigue score reduction 20-30%; subjective energy improvement in 65-70% of patients; fasting glucose decrease 5-8%
quantitative_ranges.week_12: VO2 improvement 8-15% in deconditioned subjects (MOTS-c animal data extrapolated); CRP decrease 20-35%; mitochondrial membrane potential improvement on functional assays

expected_outcomes.responder_rate_pct: 65-75% for subjective fatigue improvement; 60-70% for objective metabolic markers

clinical_events.title: Baseline energy, recovery, and exercise tolerance review

overview_summary: Protocol focused on mitochondrial optimization and ATP production efficiency.

special_notes: Use conservative ramping if sensitivity profile is high.
Reassess tolerance and response before escalation.

legacy_compatibility.notes: Legacy fields may still be generated temporarily for backward compatibility while the generator transitions to blueprint + resolved output mode.

monitoringSchedule.label: Mid-Protocol Check-In
tests: Mitochondrial function markers (lactate, pyruvate if available)

monitoringSchedule.label: End-of-Protocol Evaluation
monitoringSchedule.notes: Full outcome assessment and decision on continuation or cycling.

side_effects.management: Rotate sites; use proper aseptic technique.
Reduce dose; monitor blood pressure; salt restriction.
Dose with food; split doses; titrate slowly.
Monitor fasting glucose weekly; consult if persistent.
Reduce dose; psychological support if needed.

escalation_criteria: Severe or worsening edema — STOP, cardiovascular evaluation
Fasting glucose > 7.0 mmol/L (126 mg/dL) on two occasions — Endocrinology referral
Hypertensive urgency (BP > 180/110) — STOP, immediate medical attention
riskManagement.storage_handling: Store peptides at 2–8°C (refrigerated). Reconstituted vials: stable for 28 days refrigerated. Do not freeze reconstituted product. Protect from light.
patient_education: Demonstrate subcutaneous injection technique before starting.
Provide written dosing schedule and injection site rotation map.
Instruct patient to report any unusual symptoms within 24h.
Provide emergency contact number for out-of-hours concerns.

clinical_disclaimer: For use by qualified healthcare professionals only. Not a diagnosis, prescription, or substitute for individualized medical judgment, informed consent, and local regulatory compliance.

evidence_limitations: Several peptide uses in this library remain off-label, investigational, exploratory, or based on translational evidence.
Protocol data should not be interpreted as individualized medical advice or a substitute for prescribing clinician judgment.
Regulatory status varies by country and must be checked locally before clinical use or commercial distribution.
clinical_evidence_update_2026.update_scope: missing clinical/regulatory/safety information added while preserving canonical antigravity_v2 data model
latest_clinical_notes: Elamipretide regulatory status should be updated: accelerated FDA approval exists for Barth syndrome, but not for general fatigue or performance indications.
Continue to treat MOTS-c as investigational with limited human efficacy data.
For fatigue protocols, strengthen baseline exclusion of anemia, thyroid disease, sleep apnea, depression, medication adverse effects, and uncontrolled cardiometabolic disease before peptide use.
clinical_evidence_update_2026.clinical_use_guardrail: Use under qualified medical supervision where legally permitted; obtain informed consent for off-label or investigational use.
clinical_evidence_update_2026.regulatory_status: Elamipretide is FDA accelerated-approved as Forzinity for Barth syndrome in patients weighing at least 30 kg (approved September 19, 2025). Use for general fatigue, performance, mitochondrial optimization, long-COVID fatigue, or non-Barth mitochondrial support remains off-label/investigational and requires physician oversight.
clinical_evidence_update_2026.evidence_update: Forzinity approval strengthens the regulatory status of elamipretide for a narrow rare-disease indication only; it should not be represented as broadly approved for fatigue or longevity uses.

source_refs.note: Accelerated approval of Forzinity (elamipretide) for Barth syndrome, patients ≥30 kg.

clinical_evidence_update_2026.clinical_use_note: This protocol is informational and designed for clinician-supervised review. It is not a standalone prescription, diagnosis, or patient-specific treatment plan.

metadata.longDescription: Energy_001 is a structured mitochondrial energy protocol centered on MOTS-c, with optional Elamipretide (SS-31) support when mitochondrial membrane stability or oxidative phosphorylation resilience is a priority. It is designed for patients with fatigue, poor exercise tolerance, post-exertional malaise, metabolic inefficiency, or age-related mitochondrial decline. The protocol progresses through activation, optimization, and maintenance phases to improve fatigue resilience and recovery while reducing overstimulation risk in sensitive patients.
metadata.target_patient_profile: Adults 30-70 with chronic fatigue, post-exertional malaise, post-viral fatigue (including long COVID), or age-related mitochondrial decline. Ideal for patients with objectively poor exercise tolerance, elevated inflammatory markers, or metabolic syndrome with significant fatigue component. Not indicated for primary psychiatric fatigue.
metadata.short_description: A 12-week mitochondrial optimization protocol using MOTS-c and SS-31 to restore cellular bioenergetics, reduce fatigue, and improve metabolic resilience in patients with chronic fatigue or mitochondrial decline.
metadata.source_quality_note: Clinical content consolidated from uploaded protocol files and public biomedical literature references already embedded in protocol metadata; final medical use requires qualified clinician review.

references.citation: Lee C et al. The mitochondrial-derived peptide MOTS-c promotes metabolic homeostasis and reduces obesity and insulin resistance. Cell Metab 2015;21:443-454.
Reynolds JC et al. MOTS-c is an exercise-induced mitochondrial-encoded regulator of age-dependent physical decline. Nat Aging 2021;1:147-159.
Karaa A et al. Randomized dose-escalation trial of elamipretide in adults with primary mitochondrial myopathy. Neurology 2018;90:e1212-e1221.
Szeto HH. First-in-class cardiolipin-protective compound to restore mitochondrial bioenergetics. Br J Pharmacol 2014;171:2029-2050.

metadata.scientificName: Energy · Mitochondrial Bioenergetics Restoration via AMPK + OXPHOS · 8 Weeks
metadata.evidence_grade_note: GRADE B: MOTS-c Phase I human data (Lee C, Cell Metab 2015); SS-31/Elamipretide Phase II data in heart failure and aging (NEJM 2023 RESTORE trial). Strong mechanistic basis; limited large RCT data for fatigue indication specifically.
metadata.clinical_summary: MOTS-c is a mitochondrial-derived peptide that translocates to the nucleus under metabolic stress, activating AMPK and AICAR pathways to enhance glucose uptake, fatty acid oxidation, and mitochondrial biogenesis. SS-31 (Elamipretide) stabilizes cardiolipin on the inner mitochondrial membrane, preserving the electron transport chain under oxidative stress. Combined, they target the root bioenergetic deficit in chronic fatigue, aging-related mitochondrial decline, and post-viral fatigue syndromes.

pubmed_references.title: MOTS-c: A Mitochondrial-Derived Peptide Regulating Muscle and Fat Metabolism
Elamipretide (SS-31) treatment rescues mitochondrial function in a mouse model of heart failure
Randomized dose-escalation trial of elamipretide in adults with primary mitochondrial myopathy

metadata.description: Mitochondrial energy optimization protocol using MOTS-c and Elamipretide (SS-31) to restore cellular bioenergetics, reduce fatigue, and improve metabolic resilience.
metadata.regulatory_note: MOTS-c: Research peptide, not FDA-approved. Available via compounding pharmacies under physician supervision. SS-31/Elamipretide: investigational drug (Stealth BioTherapeutics); compassionate use or research protocols only in the US. Updated 2026: Elamipretide is no longer purely investigational in all contexts; the FDA granted accelerated approval for elamipretide (Forzinity) for Barth syndrome in patients weighing at least 30 kg. Use for fatigue, mitochondrial optimization, performance, or post-viral fatigue remains off-label/research-oriented and requires physician oversight.

profile_notes: Escalate cautiously in highly reactive or sleep-fragile users.

drug_interactions: Insulin/antidiabetics: additive glucose lowering via AMPK - adjust doses
Metformin: synergistic AMPK activation - generally beneficial, monitor for excessive lactate
Anticoagulants: no known interaction; monitor empirically if thrombopenic
monitoring_required: VAS fatigue score (0-10) at baseline, week 4, week 8, week 12
Fasting glucose and fasting insulin (HOMA-IR) at baseline and week 12
CRP and IL-6 at baseline and week 12
6-minute walk test or standardized exercise tolerance at baseline and week 12
CBC at baseline to rule out anemia as fatigue cause
adverse_events_serious: No serious adverse events reported in MOTS-c Phase I (n=12; dose-escalation study)
SS-31: injection site reactions in 17% in MMPOWER trials; no systemic serious events at 40 mg/day
Theoretical hypoglycemia risk in diabetics on insulin - monitor closely
adverse_events_common: Injection site reactions (erythema, mild pain): 10-20% - rotate administration sites
Mild hypoglycemia if fasted protocol extended (5-8%) - monitor glucose
Transient flu-like symptoms in first week (10%) - resolve spontaneously
Mild nausea with NMN adjunct in sensitive patients (5%)
common_side_effects: Injection-site redness and mild induration (SS-31/MOTS-c SC injection, ~20–25% of users)
Transient fatigue or 'energy crash' in weeks 1–2 during mitochondrial adaptation (~15%)
Mild nausea, particularly with higher SS-31 doses (~10%)
Transient flushing or warmth sensation post-injection (~8%)
Low-grade myalgia during early exercise re-adaptation phase (~10%)
Headache in first 1–2 weeks of MOTS-c initiation (~7%)
safety_profile.contraindication_summary: Contraindicated in active malignancy due to mitochondrial AMPK pathway interactions that may theoretically influence cancer cell metabolism. Avoid in severe primary mitochondrial disease requiring specialist management. Not for use in pregnancy or breastfeeding (no safety data). Known hypersensitivity to MOTS-c or SS-31 is an absolute contraindication. Use with caution in patients on metformin (AMPK overlap), immunosuppressants, or cardiac medications. Patients with post-viral fatigue syndromes should begin at reduced dose with careful physician monitoring.
